name: tessera-playtest-orchestrator
description: Self-contained multi-role playtest. A single claude-code agent creates a Tessera game via POST /api/games (capturing code + host_token), then opens up to 10 Playwright browser contexts — one per role (1 GM + 3 builders + 3 guiders + 3 observers) — and drives them through a full round in parallel. Findings are aggregated server-side by the same agent and emitted as one JSON object on stdout. No Jetty fan-out, no list_emit_await, no extract_from_trajectories — single trajectory, single result.

play.instruction:
You are running a **10-role concurrent playtest** of Tessera, a no-login facilitation game. You will: create the game yourself, open up to 10 Playwright browser contexts (one per role), drive them through a full round simultaneously, and emit a single aggregated JSON report.

**The game URL is `https://tessera.schaffters.com`.** All API calls and navigation target that origin.

## Phase 0: Setup

1. `npx --yes playwright install chromium` (the prism-playwright snapshot is missing the binary — install before any browser tool).
2. Test the API surface with a quick curl: `curl -sS https://tessera.schaffters.com/` should return HTML (200).

## Phase 1: Create the game

POST to `/api/games` to create a game directly (no UI needed):

```sh
curl -sS -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "workshop_name": "Orchestrator playtest",
    "video_call_url": "https://meet.example.com/orchestrator-playtest",
    "team_mode": "gm_picks",
    "default_complexity": 5,
    "round_count": 1,
    "round_duration_seconds": 600,
    "participant_cap": 16,
    "builder_brief_on": true,
    "guider_brief_on": true,
    "builder_brief_source": "library",
    "guider_brief_source": "library",
    "sound_on": true
  }' \
  https://tessera.schaffters.com/api/games
```

The response is `{"code": "ABC-123", "host_token": "..."}`. Save BOTH — you need them. The code goes in URLs; the host_token lets the GM tab claim the master session via `/host-recover/<CODE>`.

## Phase 2: Open 10 Playwright contexts

Using the Playwright MCP, open **one browser, ten contexts**. Each context is an isolated cookie jar so role sessions don't bleed into each other. Suggested roster:

| Idx | Role     | Name        | Pair |
|-----|----------|-------------|------|
| 0   | gm       | Facilitator | —    |
| 1   | builder  | Avery       | 0    |
| 2   | guider   | Bri         | 0    |
| 3   | builder  | Cameron     | 1    |
| 4   | guider   | Drew        | 1    |
| 5   | builder  | Ellis       | 2    |
| 6   | guider   | Finley      | 2    |
| 7   | observer | Gray        | 0    |
| 8   | observer | Harper      | 1    |
| 9   | observer | Indigo      | 2    |

For each context, capture `joined_at` timestamp before navigating.

## Phase 3: Wire each role into the game

### GM tab (idx 0)
1. Navigate to `https://tessera.schaffters.com/host-recover/<CODE>`.
2. Paste the host_token in the form, submit. You should land on `/g/<CODE>/master`.
3. (Alternative if the form is finicky: from a navigated tessera page, run in console: `await fetch('/api/games/<CODE>/host-recover', {method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({token:'<TOKEN>'})})`. Cookies attach automatically. Then refresh `/g/<CODE>/master`.)
4. Wait until the lobby panel shows 9 players. Don't rush — let players join in their own tabs first.

### Player tabs (idx 1–9)
For each: navigate to `https://tessera.schaffters.com/g/<CODE>/join`, fill display name = `<assigned name>`, submit. They land in `/g/<CODE>/play` with role=lobby.

## Phase 4: GM allocates + starts the round

In the GM tab:
1. In the auto-allocate block, set the `pairs` numeric input to `3`, click **Create 3 pairs**.
2. Click **Auto-assign 3 observers** (it lights up once pairs exist + lobby members remain).
3. Set complexity to `5` (+/− stepper) and duration to `10` minutes (free-text input next to the start button). Click **Start round 1**.

The round timer starts. From here you have 10 wall-clock minutes (the round duration) to drive all the player tabs through their playbooks.

## Phase 5: Per-role playbook (concurrent)

Multiplex your attention across all tabs during the round. Aim for these activities per role:

### Builders (Avery, Cameron, Ellis)
- Open the sealed brief envelope (tap the seal). Confirm the gate drops.
- Place ~10–14 pieces using a mix of shapes (sq, tri-up, rhomb, trap) and colors. Sample latency on 3 placements (time from click to piece visible).
- Tap **Test solution** at least twice — once early (≥3 placements), once late (≥10).
- Try **Clear all** once: tap, observe `Tap again to clear` arming, tap again, confirm wipe.
- Watch for the pair-name nudge modal (should pop after closing the brief if pair is unnamed). Click `skip for now`.

### Guiders (Bri, Drew, Finley)
- Open the brief envelope, observe the goal pattern at complexity 5.
- Click the PairNameBadge (yellow-bordered when unnamed) within the first 60s and rename the pair (e.g. "The Pelicans").
- Try minimizing the brief (the `−` button) and re-expanding (click the seal circle).

### Observers (Gray, Harper, Indigo)
- Confirm side-by-side builder + goal layout renders within ~3s of round start.
- Capture the time from join to first goal-canvas mount.
- If multiple pairs are available, switch to a different pair at least once.

### GM (Facilitator)
- Switch focused pair every ~90s by clicking pairs in the PairsPanel.
- Note pair-switching delay, super-power broadcast latency, allocation UX.

## Phase 6: Round end + game end

When the timer hits 0:
1. Round ends automatically. All tabs see the RoundEndedView.
2. In the GM tab, click **End game** (or wait for round_count=1 to terminate).
3. Capture the leaderboard.

## Phase 7: Aggregate findings + emit JSON

Across all 10 tabs, collate:
- Console errors (devtools across each context).
- Network errors (failed requests).
- UX surprises per role.
- Latency samples (place-to-visible, broadcast-to-UI).
- Confirmations of expected behaviour (test solution scoring, brief gate, pair-rename realtime, etc.).

**Dedupe across players.** If 3 builders all flag 'place-piece feels laggy', that's one finding with 3 corroborators, not three.

## Final output (stdout JSON)

The LAST thing you print MUST be a single JSON object matching this schema. Anything else — narrative, scratch — goes ABOVE the JSON.

```json
{
  "scenario_id": "orchestrator-c5-1round-10roles",
  "game_code": "<CODE you created>",
  "duration_sec": <int>,
  "outcome": "passed | partial | failed",
  "executive_summary": "<one paragraph>",
  "per_role_summary": {
    "gm": "...",
    "builder": "...",
    "guider": "...",
    "observer": "..."
  },
  "latency_samples_ms": {
    "place_to_visible": [<int>, ...],
    "broadcast_to_ui_update": [<int>, ...],
    "pair_rename_propagation": [<int>, ...]
  },
  "concurrency_findings": [
    "<latency or fan-out observation>"
  ],
  "scaling_findings": [
    "<server-side perf or load observation with 10 active sockets>"
  ],
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "blocker | major | minor | nit",
      "role": "gm | builder | guider | observer | shared",
      "category": "bug | ux-confusion | slowness | copy | accessibility | visual | performance",
      "title": "Short headline",
      "detail": "What you observed and where",
      "corroborators": <int>,
      "evidence": "screenshot path or console excerpt"
    }
  ],
  "console_errors": ["<deduped console messages>"],
  "network_errors": ["<failed-request URLs>"]
}
```

**Outcome rules:** `passed` = no blockers, ≤2 majors. `partial` = no blockers, 3+ majors OR any console error. `failed` = ≥1 blocker OR you did not complete the round.

**Memory budget:** 10 chromium contexts ≈ 2GB. Stay under 7GB total — close any context you've finished observing if you start hitting memory pressure. The container has 8GB available.
